'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}}) - Listed as a special shrine.

{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪市}}）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県{{ill|Chino|ja|茅野市}}）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}}) - Listed as a special shrine.

{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]） | image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[茅野市]]） | image3 = 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3 = 下社春宮（長野県[[下諏訪町]]） | image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer = 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮 }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、「諏訪」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。 == 概要 == 全国に約25,000社あり、[[長野県]]の[[諏訪湖]]近くの'''[[諏訪大社]]'''（旧称：諏訪神社）を総本社とする。また、諏訪神社を中心とする[[神道]]の信仰を'''諏訪信仰'''（すわしんこう）という[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0-85378 『諏訪信仰』] - [[コトバンク]][https://suwa-tabi.jp/suwa-shinbutsupj/about/ 諏訪信仰について - 諏訪神仏プロジェクト]。諏訪信仰は日本全国に広まっている。 諏訪大社の祭神は「諏訪大明神」ともいわれる'''[[建御名方神]]'''とその[[妃]]・'''[[八坂刀売神]]'''で、他の諏訪神社もこの2神を主祭神とするほか、「諏訪大神」と総称することもある。諏訪大社より祭神を勧請する際には[[薙鎌]]に神霊が移され、各神社ではこれを[[神体]]としている。また、中世には狩猟神事を執り行っていたことから、[[狩猟]]、[[漁業]]を守護する神社としても崇拝を受ける「大法輪」第72巻1号、[[法藏館]]、90頁、2005年。これらは諏訪の神の[[山の神|山神]]としての性格を表している。 諏訪大社では6年に一度、'''御柱'''（おんばしら）と呼ばれる4本の柱を立てる'''[[御柱祭]]'''が行われるが、同様の祭が行われる諏訪神社が多い。また、諏訪大社の春の'''[[御頭祭]]'''（おんとうさい）や、8月下旬（元は7月下旬）に行われる狩猟神事'''[[御射山祭]]'''（みさやまさい）にちなんだ祭事を行うところもある。 {{main|諏訪大社#年間祭事}} [[岡田莊司]]らによると、祭神で全国の神社を分類すれば、諏訪信仰に分類される神社は全国6位（2,616社）であるという。 == 主な諏訪神社の一覧 == === 総本社 === * '''[[諏訪大社]]'''（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]、[[茅野市]]、[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） - 諏訪神社総本社 === 北海道 === * [[諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]（[[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]） * [[花磯諏訪神社]]（[[爾志郡]][[乙部町]]） === 東北地方 === [[ファイル:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|サムネイル|[[秋田諏訪宮]]]] ; 青森県 * [[諏訪神社 (青森市)]]（[[青森市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)]]（[[三戸郡]][[南部町 (青森県)|南部町]]） ; 岩手県 * 諏訪神社（[[花巻市]]上諏訪） * [[諏訪神社 (北上市)]]（[[北上市]]諏訪町） * 諏訪神社（[[久慈市]]長内町） - [[源義経]]の北方伝説ゆかりの伝承が残っている。 ; 宮城県 * [[諏訪神社 (仙台市太白区)]]（[[仙台市]][[太白区]]） * [[諏訪神社 (仙台市青葉区)]]（仙台市[[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]） ; 秋田県 * [[秋田諏訪宮]]（[[仙北郡]][[美郷町 (秋田県)|美郷町]]） - [[別表神社]] ; 山形県 * [[諏訪神社 (山形市)]]（[[山形市]]諏訪町） ; 福島県 * [[諏方神社 (会津若松市)]]（[[会津若松市]]） * [[小名浜諏訪神社]]（[[いわき市]]小名浜諏訪町） * 諏訪神社（いわき市小川町） * [[北宮諏方神社]]（[[喜多方市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (小野町)]]（[[田村郡]][[小野町]]） === 関東地方 === ; | image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町}})

; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}}) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Mishaguji|ja|ミシャグジ}} * {{ill|Chikuma Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}}

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}}) - Listed as a special shrine.

{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪市}}）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県{{ill|Chino|ja|茅野市}}）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}}) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Mishaguji|ja|ミシャグジ}} * {{ill|Chikuma Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}}

revision.text:
{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪市}}）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県{{ill|Chino|ja|茅野市}}）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}}) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Mishaguji|ja|ミシャグジ}} * {{ill|Chikuma Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}}

| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.

; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})

=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}}) - Listed as a special shrine.

{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪市}}）

| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}}) - Listed as a special shrine.

Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市}}) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Mishaguji|ja|ミシャグジ}} * {{ill|Chikuma Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}}

{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}{{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪市|12=draft}}）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県{{ill|Chino|ja|茅野市|12=draft}}）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=draft}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神|12=draft}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=draft}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=draft}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=draft}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町|12=draft}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=draft}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=draft}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市|12=draft}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|12=draft}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市|12=draft}}) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishaguji|ja|ミシャグジ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chikuma Shrine|ja|生島足島神社|12=draft}}

{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]） | image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[茅野市]]） | image3 = 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3 = 下社春宮（長野県[[下諏訪町]]） | image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer = 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮 }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、「諏訪」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。 == 概要 == 全国に約25,000社あり、[[長野県]]の[[諏訪湖]]近くの'''[[諏訪大社]]'''（旧称：諏訪神社）を総本社とする。また、諏訪神社を中心とする[[神道]]の信仰を'''諏訪信仰'''（すわしんこう）という[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0-85378 『諏訪信仰』] - [[コトバンク]][https://suwa-tabi.jp/suwa-shinbutsupj/about/ 諏訪信仰について - 諏訪神仏プロジェクト]。諏訪信仰は日本全国に広まっている。 諏訪大社の祭神は「諏訪大明神」ともいわれる'''[[建御名方神]]'''とその[[妃]]・'''[[八坂刀売神]]'''で、他の諏訪神社もこの2神を主祭神とするほか、「諏訪大神」と総称することもある。諏訪大社より祭神を勧請する際には[[薙鎌]]に神霊が移され、各神社ではこれを[[神体]]としている。また、中世には狩猟神事を執り行っていたことから、[[狩猟]]、[[漁業]]を守護する神社としても崇拝を受ける「大法輪」第72巻1号、[[法藏館]]、90頁、2005年。これらは諏訪の神の[[山の神|山神]]としての性格を表している。 諏訪大社では6年に一度、'''御柱'''（おんばしら）と呼ばれる4本の柱を立てる'''[[御柱祭]]'''が行われるが、同様の祭が行われる諏訪神社が多い。また、諏訪大社の春の'''[[御頭祭]]'''（おんとうさい）や、8月下旬（元は7月下旬）に行われる狩猟神事'''[[御射山祭]]'''（みさやまさい）にちなんだ祭事を行うところもある。 {{main|諏訪大社#年間祭事}} [[岡田莊司]]らによると、祭神で全国の神社を分類すれば、諏訪信仰に分類される神社は全国6位（2,616社）であるという。 == 主な諏訪神社の一覧 == === 総本社 === * '''[[諏訪大社]]'''（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]、[[茅野市]]、[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） - 諏訪神社総本社 === 北海道 === * [[諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]（[[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]） * [[花磯諏訪神社]]（[[爾志郡]][[乙部町]]） === 東北地方 === [[ファイル:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|サムネイル|[[秋田諏訪宮]]]] ; 青森県 * [[諏訪神社 (青森市)]]（[[青森市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)]]（[[三戸郡]][[南部町 (青森県)|南部町]]） ; 岩手県 * 諏訪神社（[[花巻市]]上諏訪） * [[諏訪神社 (北上市)]]（[[北上市]]諏訪町） * 諏訪神社（[[久慈市]]長内町） - [[源義経]]の北方伝説ゆかりの伝承が残っている。 ; 宮城県 * [[諏訪神社 (仙台市太白区)]]（[[仙台市]][[太白区]]） * [[諏訪神社 (仙台市青葉区)]]（仙台市[[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]） ; 秋田県 * [[秋田諏訪宮]]（[[仙北郡]][[美郷町 (秋田県)|美郷町]]） - [[別表神社]] ; 山形県 * [[諏訪神社 (山形市)]]（[[山形市]]諏訪町） ; 福島県 * [[諏方神社 (会津若松市)]]（[[会津若松市]]） * [[小名浜諏訪神社]]（[[いわき市]]小名浜諏訪町） * 諏訪神社（いわき市小川町） * [[北宮諏方神社]]（[[喜多方市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (小野町)]]（[[田村郡]][[小野町]]） === 関東地方 === ; 茨城県 * [[結城諏訪神社]]（[[結城市]]大字上山川） * [[諏訪神社 (常総市)]]（[[常総市]]水海道諏訪町） * [[諏訪神社 (日立市)]]（[[日立市]]諏訪町） ; 群馬県 * [[諏訪神社 (藤岡市)]]（[[藤岡市]]藤岡） ; 埼玉県 * [[諏訪神社 (飯能市)]]（[[飯能市]]下名栗） * [[血洗島#諏訪神社]]（[[深谷市]][[血洗島]]） * [[諏訪神社 (深谷市上柴町)]]（深谷市上柴町） * [[諏訪神社 (加須市諏訪)]]（[[加須市]]諏訪） * [[諏訪神社 (加須市串作)]]（加須市串作） * [[諏訪神社 (川越市)]]（[[川越市]]藤間） * [[諏訪神社 (さいたま市見沼区南中野)]]（[[さいたま市]][[見沼区]][[南中野 (さいたま市)|南中野]]) * [[諏訪社 (さいたま市桜区)]]（さいたま市[[桜区]]） * [[諏訪神社 (さいたま市岩槻区諏訪)]]（さいたま市[[岩槻区]]諏訪） * [[諏訪神社 (久喜市下清久)]]（[[久喜市]]下清久） * [[諏訪神社 (久喜市太田袋)]]（久喜市太田袋） * [[諏訪神社 (上尾市)]]（[[上尾市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (桶川市)]]（[[桶川市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (羽生市稲子)]]（[[羽生市]]稲子） ; {{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}{{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪市|12=draft}}）

| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=draft}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神|12=draft}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=draft}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=draft}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=draft}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町|12=draft}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=draft}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=draft}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).

{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}{{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪市|12=draft}}）

| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町|12=draft}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=draft}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神|12=draft}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=draft}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=draft}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=draft}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町|12=draft}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=draft}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=draft}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市|12=draft}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|12=draft}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市|12=draft}}) - Listed as a special shrine.

{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]） | image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[茅野市]]） | image3 = 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3 = 下社春宮（長野県[[下諏訪町]]） | image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer = 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮 }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、「諏訪」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。 == 概要 == 全国に約25,000社あり、[[長野県]]の[[諏訪湖]]近くの'''[[諏訪大社]]'''（旧称：諏訪神社）を総本社とする。また、諏訪神社を中心とする[[神道]]の信仰を'''諏訪信仰'''（すわしんこう）という[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0-85378 『諏訪信仰』] - [[コトバンク]][https://suwa-tabi.jp/suwa-shinbutsupj/about/ 諏訪信仰について - 諏訪神仏プロジェクト]。諏訪信仰は日本全国に広まっている。 諏訪大社の祭神は「諏訪大明神」ともいわれる'''[[建御名方神]]'''とその[[妃]]・'''[[八坂刀売神]]'''で、他の諏訪神社もこの2神を主祭神とするほか、「諏訪大神」と総称することもある。諏訪大社より祭神を勧請する際には[[薙鎌]]に神霊が移され、各神社ではこれを[[神体]]としている。また、中世には狩猟神事を執り行っていたことから、[[狩猟]]、[[漁業]]を守護する神社としても崇拝を受ける「大法輪」第72巻1号、[[法藏館]]、90頁、2005年。これらは諏訪の神の[[山の神|山神]]としての性格を表している。 諏訪大社では6年に一度、'''御柱'''（おんばしら）と呼ばれる4本の柱を立てる'''[[御柱祭]]'''が行われるが、同様の祭が行われる諏訪神社が多い。また、諏訪大社の春の'''[[御頭祭]]'''（おんとうさい）や、8月下旬（元は7月下旬）に行われる狩猟神事'''[[御射山祭]]'''（みさやまさい）にちなんだ祭事を行うところもある。 {{main|諏訪大社#年間祭事}} [[岡田莊司]]らによると、祭神で全国の神社を分類すれば、諏訪信仰に分類される神社は全国6位（2,616社）であるという。 == 主な諏訪神社の一覧 == === 総本社 === * '''[[諏訪大社]]'''（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]、[[茅野市]]、[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） - 諏訪神社総本社 === 北海道 === * [[諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]（[[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]） * [[花磯諏訪神社]]（[[爾志郡]][[乙部町]]） === 東北地方 === [[ファイル:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|サムネイル|[[秋田諏訪宮]]]] ; Immanuelle moved page [[諏訪神社]] to [[Suwa Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]） | image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[茅野市]]） | image3 = 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3 = 下社春宮（長野県[[下諏訪町]]） | image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer = 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮 }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、「諏訪」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。 == 概要 == 全国に約25,000社あり、[[長野県]]の[[諏訪湖]]近くの'''[[諏訪大社]]'''（旧称：諏訪神社）を総本社とする。また、諏訪神社を中心とする[[神道]]の信仰を'''諏訪信仰'''（すわしんこう）という[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E4%BF%A1%E4%BB%B0-85378 『諏訪信仰』] - [[コトバンク]][https://suwa-tabi.jp/suwa-shinbutsupj/about/ 諏訪信仰について - 諏訪神仏プロジェクト]。諏訪信仰は日本全国に広まっている。 諏訪大社の祭神は「諏訪大明神」ともいわれる'''[[建御名方神]]'''とその[[妃]]・'''[[八坂刀売神]]'''で、他の諏訪神社もこの2神を主祭神とするほか、「諏訪大神」と総称することもある。諏訪大社より祭神を勧請する際には[[薙鎌]]に神霊が移され、各神社ではこれを[[神体]]としている。また、中世には狩猟神事を執り行っていたことから、[[狩猟]]、[[漁業]]を守護する神社としても崇拝を受ける「大法輪」第72巻1号、[[法藏館]]、90頁、2005年。これらは諏訪の神の[[山の神|山神]]としての性格を表している。 諏訪大社では6年に一度、'''御柱'''（おんばしら）と呼ばれる4本の柱を立てる'''[[御柱祭]]'''が行われるが、同様の祭が行われる諏訪神社が多い。また、諏訪大社の春の'''[[御頭祭]]'''（おんとうさい）や、8月下旬（元は7月下旬）に行われる狩猟神事'''[[御射山祭]]'''（みさやまさい）にちなんだ祭事を行うところもある。 {{main|諏訪大社#年間祭事}} [[岡田莊司]]らによると、祭神で全国の神社を分類すれば、諏訪信仰に分類される神社は全国6位（2,616社）であるという。 == 主な諏訪神社の一覧 == === 総本社 === * '''[[諏訪大社]]'''（[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]、[[茅野市]]、[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） - 諏訪神社総本社 === 北海道 === * [[諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]（[[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]） * [[花磯諏訪神社]]（[[爾志郡]][[乙部町]]） === 東北地方 === [[ファイル:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|サムネイル|[[秋田諏訪宮]]]] ; 青森県 * [[諏訪神社 (青森市)]]（[[青森市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)]]（[[三戸郡]][[南部町 (青森県)|南部町]]） ; 岩手県 * 諏訪神社（[[花巻市]]上諏訪） * [[諏訪神社 (北上市)]]（[[北上市]]諏訪町） * 諏訪神社（[[久慈市]]長内町） - [[源義経]]の北方伝説ゆかりの伝承が残っている。 ; 宮城県 * [[諏訪神社 (仙台市太白区)]]（[[仙台市]][[太白区]]） * [[諏訪神社 (仙台市青葉区)]]（仙台市[[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]） ; 秋田県 * [[秋田諏訪宮]]（[[仙北郡]][[美郷町 (秋田県)|美郷町]]） - [[別表神社]] ; 山形県 * [[諏訪神社 (山形市)]]（[[山形市]]諏訪町） ; 福島県 * [[諏方神社 (会津若松市)]]（[[会津若松市]]） * [[小名浜諏訪神社]]（[[いわき市]]小名浜諏訪町） * 諏訪神社（いわき市小川町） * [[北宮諏方神社]]（[[喜多方市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (小野町)]]（[[田村郡]][[小野町]]） === 関東地方 === ; 茨城県 * [[結城諏訪神社]]（[[結城市]]大字上山川） * [[諏訪神社 (常総市)]]（[[常総市]]水海道諏訪町） * [[諏訪神社 (日立市)]]（[[日立市]]諏訪町） ; 群馬県 * [[諏訪神社 (藤岡市)]]（[[藤岡市]]藤岡） ; 埼玉県 * [[諏訪神社 (飯能市)]]（[[飯能市]]下名栗） * [[血洗島#諏訪神社]]（[[深谷市]][[血洗島]]） * [[諏訪神社 (深谷市上柴町)]]（深谷市上柴町） * [[諏訪神社 (加須市諏訪)]]（[[加須市]]諏訪） * [[諏訪神社 (加須市串作)]]（加須市串作） * [[諏訪神社 (川越市)]]（[[川越市]]藤間） * [[諏訪神社 (さいたま市見沼区南中野)]]（[[さいたま市]][[見沼区]][[南中野 (さいたま市)|南中野]]) * [[諏訪社 (さいたま市桜区)]]（さいたま市[[桜区]]） * [[諏訪神社 (さいたま市岩槻区諏訪)]]（さいたま市[[岩槻区]]諏訪） * [[諏訪神社 (久喜市下清久)]]（[[久喜市]]下清久） * [[諏訪神社 (久喜市太田袋)]]（久喜市太田袋） * [[諏訪神社 (上尾市)]]（[[上尾市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (桶川市)]]（[[桶川市]]） * [[諏訪神社 (羽生市稲子)]]（[[羽生市]]稲子） ; 千葉県 * [[諏訪神社 (流山市)]]（[[流山市]][[駒木 (流山市)|駒木]]） * [[諏訪神社 (柏市)]]（[[柏市]][[柏 (柏市)|柏]]） * [[諏訪神社 (市川市)]]（[[市川市]][[平田 (市川市)|平田]]） * [[諏訪神社 (船橋市行田)]]（[[船橋市]][[行田 (船橋市)|行田]]） * [[諏訪神社 (千葉市花見川区)]]（[[千葉市]][[花見川区]]長作町） * [[諏訪神社 (千葉市中央区浜野町)]]（千葉市[[中央区 (千葉市)|中央区]]浜野町） * [[諏訪神社 (白井市)]]（[[白井市]]根） * [[諏訪神社 (八千代市)]]（[[八千代市]]八千代台西） * [[諏訪大神 (東庄町)]]（[[香取郡]][[東庄町]]） * [[諏訪神社 (香取市)]]（[[香取市]]佐原イ） * [[諏訪神社 (いすみ市)]]（[[いすみ市]]小澤） * [[諏訪神社 (市原市原田)]]（[[市原市]]原田） * [[諏訪神社 (館山市)]]（[[館山市]]船形） ; '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) - Listed as a special shrine.

Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}})

revision.text:
{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa|qq=}}）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県{{ill|Chino|ja|茅野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chino|qq=}}）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa|qq=}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Kami|qq=}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Kami|qq=}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onbashira Festival|qq=}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|qq=}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sapporo|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-ku|qq=}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otobe|qq=}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|qq=}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori|qq=}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|qq=}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|qq=}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|qq=}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachikawa|qq=}})

=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki|qq=}}) - Listed as a special shrine.

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Kami|qq=}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Kami|qq=}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onbashira Festival|qq=}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|qq=}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa Grand Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ({{ill|Sapporo|ja|札幌市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sapporo|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashi-ku|ja|東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-ku|qq=}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nishi District|ja|爾志郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Otobe|ja|乙部町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otobe|qq=}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|qq=}} ({{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori|qq=}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|qq=}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|qq=}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|qq=}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ({{ill|Tachikawa|ja|立川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachikawa|qq=}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōmachi|ja|大町市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmachi|qq=}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|qq=}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|lt=Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|lt=Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|ja|鎮西大社諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|ja|長崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki|qq=}}) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|タケミナカタ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} * {{ill|Mishaguji|ja|ミシャグジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishaguji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|lt=Ikushimatarushima Shrine|lt=Chikuma Shrine|ja|生島足島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Shrine|qq=}}

{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]]）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県[[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神|1=Takeminakata|2=ja|3=タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Kami|qq=}}, the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|qq=}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|qq=}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:東區|Higashi-ku]]) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Nishi District, Hokkaido|Nishi District]], [[:en:Otobe, Hokkaido|Otobe]])
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|qq=}} ([[:en:Aomori|Aomori]]) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|qq=}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|qq=}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|qq=}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ([[:en:Tachikawa, Tokyo|Tachikawa]])
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]]) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|qq=}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''[[:en:Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]]''' ([[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]]) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] * [[:en:Mishaguji|Mishaguji]] * [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Chikuma Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Suwa Faith
revision.text:
{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]]）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県[[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:東區|Higashi-ku]]) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nishi District, Hokkaido|Nishi District]], [[:en:Otobe, Hokkaido|Otobe]])
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Aomori|Aomori]]) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|qq=}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|qq=}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ([[:en:Tachikawa, Tokyo|Tachikawa]])
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]]) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''[[:en:Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]]''' ([[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]]) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] * [[:en:Mishaguji|Mishaguji]] * [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Chikuma Shrine]]

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:東區|Higashi-ku]]) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nishi District, Hokkaido|Nishi District]], [[:en:Otobe, Hokkaido|Otobe]])
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Aomori|Aomori]]) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|qq=|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|qq=|1=Misato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ([[:en:Tachikawa, Tokyo|Tachikawa]])
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]]) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''[[:en:Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]]''' ([[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]]) - Listed as a special shrine.
