{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2370082]] Wikidata name: Suwa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]]）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県[[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:東區|Higashi-ku]]) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nishi District, Hokkaido|Nishi District]], [[:en:Otobe, Hokkaido|Otobe]])
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Aomori|Aomori]]) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|qq=|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|qq=|1=Misato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ([[:en:Tachikawa, Tokyo|Tachikawa]])
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]]) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''[[:en:Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]]''' ([[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]]) - Listed as a special shrine.

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Suwa-Schrein]][[fr:Sanctuaire Suwa]][[it:Santuario di Suwa]][[ja:諏訪神社]][[ko:스와 신사]][[simple:Suwa shrine]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2370082]] Wikidata name: Suwa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]]）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県[[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:東區|Higashi-ku]]) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nishi District, Hokkaido|Nishi District]], [[:en:Otobe, Hokkaido|Otobe]])
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Aomori|Aomori]]) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|1=Misato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ([[:en:Tachikawa, Tokyo|Tachikawa]])
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]]) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''[[:en:Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]]''' ([[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]]) - Listed as a special shrine.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Suwa-Schrein]][[fr:Sanctuaire Suwa]][[it:Santuario di Suwa]][[ja:諏訪神社]][[ko:스와 신사]][[simple:Suwa shrine]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2370082]] Wikidata name: Suwa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]]）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県[[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:東區|Higashi-ku]]) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nishi District, Hokkaido|Nishi District]], [[:en:Otobe, Hokkaido|Otobe]])
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Aomori|Aomori]]) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|1=Misato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ([[:en:Tachikawa, Tokyo|Tachikawa]])
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]]) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''[[:en:Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]]''' ([[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]]) - Listed as a special shrine.

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Suwa-Schrein]][[fr:Sanctuaire Suwa]][[it:Santuario di Suwa]][[ja:諏訪神社]][[ko:스와 신사]][[simple:Suwa shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Suwa-Schrein]][[fr:Sanctuaire Suwa]][[it:Santuario di Suwa]][[ja:諏訪神社]][[ko:스와 신사]][[simple:Suwa shrine]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2370082]] Wikidata name: Suwa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]][[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]]）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県[[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県[[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]]）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:東區|Higashi-ku]]) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nishi District, Hokkaido|Nishi District]], [[:en:Otobe, Hokkaido|Otobe]])
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Aomori|Aomori]]) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|1=Misato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ([[:en:Tachikawa, Tokyo|Tachikawa]])
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]]) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''[[:en:Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]]''' ([[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]]) - Listed as a special shrine.

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] worship centered on [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], the main deity, and his consort [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]], held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ([[:en:Sapporo|Sapporo]], [[:en:東區|Higashi-ku]]) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nishi District, Hokkaido|Nishi District]], [[:en:Otobe, Hokkaido|Otobe]])
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Aomori|Aomori]]) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|1=Misato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ([[:en:Tachikawa, Tokyo|Tachikawa]])
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]]) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)

| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.

=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Aomori|en|Aomori|WD=Q146790|lt=Aomori}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|1=Misato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ({{ill|Tachikawa, Tokyo|en|Tachikawa, Tokyo|WD=Q269634|lt=Tachikawa}})

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ({{ill|Sapporo|en|Sapporo|WD=Q37951|lt=Sapporo}}, {{ill|東區|en|東區|WD=Q1241231|lt=Higashi-ku}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nishi District, Hokkaido|en|Nishi District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1185873|lt=Nishi District}}, {{ill|Otobe, Hokkaido|en|Otobe, Hokkaido|WD=Q1354219|lt=Otobe}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Aomori|en|Aomori|WD=Q146790|lt=Aomori}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|1=Misato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ({{ill|Tachikawa, Tokyo|en|Tachikawa, Tokyo|WD=Q269634|lt=Tachikawa}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmachi, Nagano|en|Ōmachi, Nagano|WD=Q385375|lt=Ōmachi}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|en|Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|WD=Q84028|lt=Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|en|Nagasaki|WD=Q38234|lt=Nagasaki}}) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|Mishaguji|en|Mishaguji|WD=Q11071209|lt=Mishaguji}} * {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|WD=Q11574513|lt=Chikuma Shrine}}

| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami|1=Yasakatome}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival|1=Onbashira}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ({{ill|Sapporo|en|Sapporo|WD=Q37951|lt=Sapporo|1=Sapporo}}, {{ill|東區|en|東區|WD=Q1241231|lt=Higashi-ku|1=東區}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nishi District, Hokkaido|en|Nishi District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1185873|lt=Nishi District|1=Nishi District, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Otobe, Hokkaido|en|Otobe, Hokkaido|WD=Q1354219|lt=Otobe|1=Otobe, Hokkaido}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Aomori|en|Aomori|WD=Q146790|lt=Aomori|1=Aomori}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|1=Q11609394|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ({{ill|Tachikawa, Tokyo|en|Tachikawa, Tokyo|WD=Q269634|lt=Tachikawa|1=Tachikawa, Tokyo}})
=== Chubu Region === ; Nagano Prefecture [[File:霧降宮切久保諏訪神社 Kirihuminoya Kirikubo Suwa shrine.jpg|thumb|Kirifuri-no-Miya Kiri-kubo Suwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Kamisuwa Shrine|ja|上諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmachi, Nagano|en|Ōmachi, Nagano|WD=Q385375|lt=Ōmachi|1=Ōmachi, Nagano}}) * {{ill|Shimosuwa Shrine|ja|下諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Omachi City)
=== Kyushu Region === [[File:Nagasaki-Suwa-Shrine-1558.jpg|thumb|Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine]] ; Nagasaki Prefecture * '''{{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|en|Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)|WD=Q84028|lt=Chinzei Taisha Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Nagasaki)}}''' ({{ill|Nagasaki|en|Nagasaki|WD=Q38234|lt=Nagasaki|1=Nagasaki}}) - Listed as a special shrine.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Suwa-jinja}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|Mishaguji|en|Mishaguji|WD=Q11071209|lt=Mishaguji|1=Mishaguji}} * {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|WD=Q11574513|lt=Chikuma Shrine|1=Ikushimatarushima Shrine}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Suwa-Schrein]][[fr:Sanctuaire Suwa]][[it:Santuario di Suwa]][[ja:諏訪神社]][[ko:스와 신사]][[simple:Suwa shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Suwa-Schrein]][[fr:Sanctuaire Suwa]][[it:Santuario di Suwa]][[ja:諏訪神社]][[ko:스와 신사]][[simple:Suwa shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Suwa-Schrein]][[fr:Sanctuaire Suwa]][[it:Santuario di Suwa]][[ja:諏訪神社]][[ko:스와 신사]][[simple:Suwa shrine]]
{{multiple image | direction = vertical | width = 250 | image1 = 諏訪大社上社本宮 拝殿・拝所 - Suwa Taisha Honmiya.jpg | caption1 = 上社本宮（{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa|1=Suwa, Nagano}}）
| image2 = Suwa Taisha Maemiya Honden.jpg | caption2 = 上社前宮（長野県{{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino|1=Chino, Nagano}}）
| image3= 諏訪大社 春宮 神楽殿.JPG | caption3= 下社春宮（長野県{{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa}}）
| image4 = Suwa taisha Shimosha Akimiya , 諏訪大社 下社 秋宮 - panoramio (7).jpg | caption4 = 下社秋宮（長野県下諏訪町） | footer_align = center | footer= 諏訪大社を構成する2社4宮}}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) refers to shrines with "Suwa" in their name, particularly associated with {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, a form of {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} worship centered on {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}.
== Overview ==
There are approximately 25,000 Suwa Shrines throughout Japan, all having {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Nagano Prefecture as their head shrine. Suwa Faith revolves around the veneration of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, the main deity, and his consort {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami|1=Yasakatome}}. Together, they are referred to as the "Suwa Deities" (諏訪大神). When Suwa Grand Shrine's deities are transferred to other Suwa Shrines, their divine spirits are believed to reside within a ritual {{ill|Nagi Kama|ja|薙鎌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Kama|qq=}}, which becomes the sacred object of the shrine.
Suwa Grand Shrine conducts the renowned {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival|1=Onbashira}}, held every six years. Many Suwa Shrines across the country host similar festivals. Additionally, rituals like the spring {{ill|Ontōsai|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontōsai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Misayama Festival|ja|御射山祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a traditional hunting ceremony in late August, are celebrated at various Suwa Shrines.
== List of Main Suwa Shrines == === Head Shrine === * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} - Located in Suwa, Chino, and Shimosuwa in Nagano Prefecture.
=== Hokkaido === * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)|qq=}} ({{ill|Sapporo|en|Sapporo|WD=Q37951|lt=Sapporo|1=Sapporo}}, {{ill|東區|en|東區|WD=Q1241231|lt=Higashi-ku|1=東區}}) * {{ill|Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja|花磯諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanaiso Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nishi District, Hokkaido|en|Nishi District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1185873|lt=Nishi District|1=Nishi District, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Otobe, Hokkaido|en|Otobe, Hokkaido|WD=Q1354219|lt=Otobe|1=Otobe, Hokkaido}})
=== Tohoku Region === [[File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg|thumb|Akita Suwa Shrine]] ; Aomori Prefecture * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Aomori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Aomori|en|Aomori|WD=Q146790|lt=Aomori|1=Aomori}}) * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja|諏訪神社 (青森県南部町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Nanbu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nanbu|ja|南部町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu|1=Nambuchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126073891}})
; Akita Prefecture * '''{{ill|Akita Suwa Shrine|ja|秋田諏訪宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Suwa Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Misato|ja|美郷町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misato|1=Q11609394|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609394}}) - Listed as a special shrine (別表神社).
=== Kanto Region === ; Tokyo [[File:Suwa Shrine 20190515(3).jpg|thumb|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa, Shibasaki)]] * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|ja|諏訪神社 (立川市柴崎町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tachikawa)|qq=}} ({{ill|Tachikawa, Tokyo|en|Tachikawa, Tokyo|WD=Q269634|lt=Tachikawa|1=Tachikawa, Tokyo}})
